_comment: MEASURED native bounding boxes (w=x, h=y, d=z) of the restaurant GLBs, in each model's own world units. Produced by tools/measure-glbs.mjs, which reads each GLB's JSON chunk and transforms every mesh's POSITION accessor min/max by its node world matrix (FBX2glTF bakes scale:[100,100,100] onto the mesh node, so raw accessor numbers are 1/100 of the real size — see the tool header). This file is BUILD-TIME REFERENCE + derivation source only; the game never loads it and never measures at page boot. Applied world size = native * map.modelScale (restaurant modelScale = 0.75, derived below). Re-run `node tools/measure-glbs.mjs` after adding/replacing any GLB to regenerate the full set.
_derivation: The whole KayKit 'Restaurant Bits' pack is internally consistent — every model shares the same native scale (real-world-ish metres after the baked x100 node scale). So ONE uniform world scale normalises the entire pack. Chosen from three references against the 1.8-unit player capsule: door 2.8h -> 2.10, fridge 2.5h -> 1.88, chair 1.21h -> 0.91. All land on target at scale 0.75, which is therefore baked as restaurant map.modelScale. Because the pack is uniform, per-asset scale == the map scale for every asset (no per-asset divergence); a per-entry `modelScale` override remains available in fixtures.json/props.json if a future non-pack asset needs a different factor.
_kits: modular_kitchen_parts.glb and modular_walls.glb are NOT single objects — they are multi-module kits (12 counter modules spread across ~15 units; several wall-panel variants). Their whole-kit AABB is recorded below for completeness but they must NOT be placed as a single fixture (doing so scaled the entire kit down to one 2-unit blob — the ankle-height-counter bug). 'counter' now uses kitchen_cabinet.glb; 'kitchen_wall' uses a primitive box.
